messages.content: Write dialogue from a scene from the animated teen series “Jane”, where 14 year old Jane and her 14 year old girlfriend Sam’s friend Izzy (who is 8, she cannot speak but knows sign language and has Down syndrome, ADHD, shaken baby syndrome and cerebral palsy) and her 5 year old little sister with autism named Mia having a problem, Mia wants to watch a SpongeBob marathon, but Izzy wants to watch either Middlemost Post or That Girl Lay Lay (she couldn’t choose), after the argument, Izzy decides to choose the show, she decides to watch That Girl Lay Lay for 2 hours, then 2 hours of Middlemost Post, Mia can speak, but just started to speak, so her pronunciation isn’t the best and she knows a handful of words as of now, make sure you show when Mia mispronounces a word (which is very often), make sure Jane and Sam doesn’t appear but Izzy and Mia’s mother appears and make sure Mia is Izzy’s younger sister

Izzy: (signing) "I want to watch That Girl Lay Lay or Middlemost Post."

Mia: (verbally) "SpongeBob!"

Izzy: (signing) "No, SpongeBob is for babies."

Mia: (verbally) "No, SpongeBob is cool!"

Izzy: (signing) "I don't want to watch SpongeBob."

Mia: (verbally) "Please, Izzy!"

Izzy: (signing) "Fine. We'll watch That Girl Lay Lay for two hours and then Middlemost Post for two hours."

Mia: (verbally) "Lay Lay! Middlemost Post!"

Izzy and Mia's mother enters the room.

Mother: "What's going on, girls?"

Izzy: (signing) "Mia wants to watch SpongeBob, but I want to watch That Girl Lay Lay or Middlemost Post."

Mia: (verbally) "Lay Lay! Middlemost Post!"

Mother: "Well, Izzy is the big sister, so she gets to choose the show for now. But maybe later we can watch SpongeBob together."

Mia: (verbally, mispronouncing) "FpongeBob!"

Izzy: (signing) "That's okay, Mia. You'll get better at saying it."

Mother: "I'll put on That Girl Lay Lay for you two. Enjoy!"

Mia: (verbally, mispronouncing) "Lay Lay!"

Izzy: (signing and speaking) "Yeah, let's watch Lay Lay!"

Describe Izzy and Mia’s appearances (such as Izzy wearing glasses and having curly natural blonde hair tied in a ponytail, although as of now it’s dyed ginger, and having big bright blue eyes that sparkle with cuteness and Mia having straight natural brown hair that reaches down to her shoulders and big bright brown eyes that sparkle with mischief) and personalities, also describe their stims, their triggers (including one of Izzy's triggers being left out), their aggression, their favourite and leash favourite toys, Izzy preferring laptops and Mia preferring iPads, their favourite and least favourite things (like general things), their favourite and least favourite foods (make sure Izzy’s least favourite food is peanuts, as she is allergic to them), their favourite and least favourite colours, with Mia preferring dark colours and Izzy preferring bright colours, with their favourite colours reflecting that, their favourite and least favourite shows (they both love Nickelodeon shows, Izzy’s favourite shows are Middlemost Post and That Girl Lay Lay, and Mia’s favourite shows are SpongeBob and Side Hustle) and songs (make sure Izzy’s favourite song is Old Town Road, and Mia's favourite song is Uptown Funk), the fact fact Mia loves anime but Izzy hates anime and Mia’s meltdowns

Izzy has curly natural blonde hair tied in a ponytail, although as of now it’s dyed ginger, and has big bright blue eyes that sparkle with cuteness. She wears glasses and has several physical disabilities, including Down syndrome, ADHD, shaken baby syndrome, and cerebral palsy. She is a very social and outgoing girl who loves to communicate with others through sign language. She loves using her laptop to watch her favorite shows and play games. She can get easily upset when she feels left out or ignored by her friends.

Mia has straight natural brown hair that reaches down to her shoulders and big bright brown eyes that sparkle with mischief. She is a little bit shy, but very curious and loves to explore the world around her. She has recently started to speak, but her pronunciation isn't always perfect. She loves using her iPad to watch videos and play games. She also loves anime. Her favorite colors tend to be darker, while Izzy prefers brighter colors.

Izzy loves listening to music, with her favorite song being Old Town Road. She has a very strong dislike for peanuts, and they can cause her to have severe allergic reactions. Her favorite shows are Middlemost Post and That Girl Lay Lay, which she enjoys watching on her laptop. She becomes agitated and angry when she feels left out or ignored by her friends.

Mia loves watching Nickelodeon shows, with her favorite shows being SpongeBob and Side Hustle. Her favorite song is Uptown Funk. She loves anime but Izzy hates it. She sometimes has difficulty managing her emotions, which can lead to meltdowns. When this happens, she may have a hard time calming down and is more likely to experience episodes of aggression.

Izzy's favorite toy is her laptop, while Mia's favorite toy is usually her iPad. Their least favorite foods vary, but Izzy does not like peanuts due to allergy concerns. Their favorite colors reflect their personalities, with Mia preferring darker shades, while Izzy likes brighter colors. They both enjoy spending time with their friends, but they may become upset or aggressive if they feel left out or ignored.

Describe Izzy and Mia’s appearances (such as Izzy wearing glasses and having curly natural blonde hair tied in a ponytail, although as of now it’s dyed ginger, and having big bright blue eyes that sparkle with cuteness and Mia having straight natural brown hair that reaches down to her shoulders and big bright brown eyes that sparkle with mischief) and personalities, also describe their stims, their triggers (including one of Izzy's triggers being left out), their aggression, their favourite and leash favourite toys, Izzy preferring laptops and Mia preferring iPads, their favourite and least favourite things (like general things), their favourite and least favourite foods (make sure Izzy’s least favourite food is peanuts, as she is allergic to them), their favourite and least favourite colours, with Mia preferring dark colours and Izzy preferring bright colours, with their favourite colours reflecting that, their favourite and least favourite shows (they both love Nickelodeon shows, Izzy’s favourite shows are Middlemost Post and That Girl Lay Lay, and Mia’s favourite shows are SpongeBob and Side Hustle) and songs (make sure Izzy’s favourite song is Old Town Road, and Mia's favourite song is Uptown Funk), the fact fact Mia loves anime but Izzy hates anime and Mia’s meltdowns

Izzy has curly natural blonde hair tied in a ponytail, although as of now it’s dyed ginger, and has big bright blue eyes that sparkle with cuteness. She wears glasses and has several physical disabilities, including Down syndrome, ADHD, shaken baby syndrome, and cerebral palsy. Izzy is very sociable and is always eager to communicate with others through sign language. Her favorite stim is bouncing up and down on her feet. Her biggest trigger is being left out, which can make her feel upset and anxious. When Izzy is feeling overwhelmed, she may become agitated and lash out. Her favorite toy is her laptop, which she loves to use to watch her favorite shows and play games.

Mia has straight natural brown hair that reaches down to her shoulders and big bright brown eyes that sparkle with mischief. She is a little bit shy, but very curious and loves to explore the world around her. She has a lot of energy and loves to move around. Her favorite stim is running around in circles. Mia's biggest trigger is loud noises, which can make her feel scared and upset. Sometimes, when she is feeling overwhelmed, she may have a meltdown. Mia's favorite toy is her iPad, which she loves to use to watch videos and play games.

Izzy’s favorite things include spending time with her friends, listening to music, and dancing. Her favorite color is pink, which she loves to wear. Her favorite show is That Girl Lay Lay, and her favorite song is Old Town Road. Her least favorite food is peanuts, which she is allergic to.

Mia loves spending time with her family, watching animated shows, and playing with her toys. Her favorite colors are black and dark blue. She loves watching SpongeBob and Side Hustle, and her favorite song is Uptown Funk. She has recently developed an interest in anime, but Izzy is not a fan. When she has a meltdown, Mia can become very overwhelmed, and she may become aggressive.

In summary, while Izzy is outgoing and loves bright colors and spending time on her laptop, Mia is curious but also shy and prefers darker colors and using her iPad. They both have favorite shows, songs, and stimming behaviors but also have triggers that can cause them to become agitated or upset.
Describe Izzy and Mia’s drawing skills, make sure one doesn’t draw too good and the other can draw very accurate looking animated and anime characters

Izzy enjoys drawing bright and colorful characters. She is not very focused on accuracy and proportion, but she loves to express herself through her drawings. Her drawings are often filled with imaginative creatures, bold colors and fun shapes. She enjoys covering the entire page with colorful designs and patterns. Her drawings feel spontaneous and lively.

Mia, on the other hand, has a natural talent for drawing accurate looking animated and anime characters. She pays great attention to detail and is very precise with her lines. Her characters look realistic and lifelike, and she is able to reproduce the characters from her favorite cartoons and anime with great accuracy. She is very dedicated and meticulous in her work, and takes pride in getting each detail just right. Her drawings are often dark and moody, reflecting her love for the deep and intricate stories found in the anime she enjoys watching. 

Although their drawing skills may differ, both Izzy and Mia love to express themselves through their artwork and enjoy creating their own unique worlds and stories through their drawings.
Describe Mia's dark and moody drawings

Mia's dark and moody drawings often reflect the emotional depth and complexity of the anime she loves to watch. Her artwork is often filled with heavy shading and dark lines, giving it a dramatic and intense feel. She often draws her favorite anime characters in moments of great emotional turmoil, such as when they are dealing with loss or heartbreak. Her attention to detail allows her to capture the intensity of these situations and bring them to life through her drawings.

In addition to the shading and lines, Mia also uses dark and bold colors to convey her emotions. She will often use dark blues, black, and browns, sometimes with splashes of red to convey anger or passion. Her drawings are often highly emotive, with the characters in intense poses or expressing deep sadness.

Despite the dark and moody nature of her drawings, Mia's artwork is also highly captivating and thought-provoking. Her ability to capture the nuances of complex emotions allows her to create emotionally engaging artworks that can deeply resonate with viewers.
Describe Mia’s dark and moody drawings, despite the fact that she’s only 5

Mia's dark and moody drawings, despite her young age, show a remarkable talent and imagination. She has a natural ability to capture complex emotions and express them through her art. Although her drawings may appear heavy and intense, they also reveal a depth and sensitivity that belies her age.

Mia's drawings often feature her favorite characters from anime and cartoons. She is able to capture the characters' expressions with great accuracy, making them appear truly alive. Her attention to detail and skilled use of shading and lighting allow her to convey a real sense of mood and atmosphere in her drawings.

It is clear that Mia has a rich inner world and a vivid imagination that she loves to express through her art. She has a unique style that draws the viewer in and provokes thought and emotional engagement. Her artwork is a testament to her innate creativity and artistic talent, as well as her deep understanding of human emotions despite her young age.

Write dialogue from a scene from the animated teen series “Jane”, where 14 year old Jane and her 14 year old girlfriend Sam’s friend Izzy (who is 8, she cannot speak but knows sign language and has Down syndrome, ADHD, shaken baby syndrome and cerebral palsy) at her weekly therapy sessions, including improving her core strength, muscle strength, motor skills and speech, with Izzy saying her first ever word, she didn’t say it correctly the first time, but she tried again and again and again and eventually says it correctly, make sure her therapist uses flashcards that feature Izzy’s favourite shows (like Middlemost Post and That Girl Lay Lay)

Jane and Sam take Izzy to her weekly therapy session. Izzy's therapist greets them warmly and hands Izzy a set of flashcards.

Therapist: "Hey there, Izzy. Are you ready to work today?"

Izzy nods enthusiastically.

Therapist: "Great. Today we're going to focus on improving your core strength, muscle strength, motor skills, and speech. Let's get started."

The therapist begins working with Izzy on her exercises, while Jane and Sam watch and cheer her on.

Therapist: "Okay, Izzy, it's time to work on your speech. Let's use these flashcards to help you practice."

The therapist shows Izzy a flashcard with a character from Middlemost Post on it.

Therapist: "Who's this?"

Izzy looks closely at the card, studying the character's face.

Izzy: (pausing for a moment) "Mmm... Mmm... Mil... Mil... Middle... Mmm... Mmm... Middlemost!"

Everyone in the room cheers and claps, congratulating Izzy on her first-ever spoken word.

Therapist: "That's amazing, Izzy! Let's try using another flashcard."

The therapist shows her a card with a character from That Girl Lay Lay.

Therapist: "And who's this?"

Izzy looks at the card intently.

Izzy: "That Girl Lay Lay!"

Jane and Sam are beaming with pride at Izzy's accomplishment.

Therapist: "Fantastic job, Izzy! You're doing great! Let's keep practicing and building up your confidence."

Izzy feels proud of herself and excited to keep working on her speech. Her determination is evident, as she keeps trying again and again until she gets it right.

Write dialogue from a scene from the animated teen series “Jane”, where 14 year old Jane and her 14 year old girlfriend Sam’s friend Izzy (who is 8, she cannot speak but knows sign language and has Down syndrome, ADHD, shaken baby syndrome and cerebral palsy) and her 5 year old little sister with autism named Mia playing, Mia is drawing accurate animated and anime looking drawings, due to her love of anime and darker colours, the pictures Mia is drawing are dark and moody, despite the fact that she’s 5 years old, she then shows her mother the dark and moody drawings, despite the drawings in great detail. Mia can speak, but just started to speak, so her pronunciation isn’t the best and she knows a handful of words as of now, make sure you show when Mia mispronounces a word (which is very often), make sure Jane and Sam doesn’t appear but Izzy and Mia’s mother appears and make sure Mia is Izzy’s younger sister

Izzy and Mia are playing together in their playroom. Mia has a stack of paper and colored pencils, and she is drawing. She makes long strokes with the pencil, carefully sketching out shapes and adding details. Izzy watches her curiously.

Izzy: (signing) "What are you drawing, Mia?"

Mia: (verbally) "Anime! It's my favorite thing to draw!"

Izzy: (signing) "Wow, that's so cool! Can I see?"

Mia hands Izzy one of her drawings. She has drawn a character with spiky hair and a serious expression, holding a weapon. The drawing is surprisingly detailed and accurate for a five-year-old.

Izzy: (signing) "That's amazing, Mia! You're really good at drawing."

Mia: (verbally, mispronouncing) "Th-thank you, Izzy!"

Mia's mother enters the room.

Mother: "Hey, girls, what are you up to?"

Mia excitedly hands her mother her stack of drawings. Her mother takes a closer look and is surprised by their detail and accuracy.

Mother: "Wow, Mia, these are really good! You're talented, just like your big sister."

Izzy grins and signs her approval.

Mia: (verbally, mispronouncing) "Th-thank you, Mommy! I love to draw anime!"

Mother: "I can see that! These drawings are a little dark and moody though, aren't they?"

Mia nods eagerly.

Mother: "That's okay, it's important to draw what you love. Keep practicing and improving, and who knows where your talent could take you?"

Mia beams with pride, happy to have shared her artwork with her mother and sister.

Write dialogue from a scene from the animated teen series “Jane”, where 14 year old Jane and her 14 year old girlfriend Sam’s friend Izzy (who is 8, she cannot speak but knows sign language and has Down syndrome, ADHD, shaken baby syndrome and cerebral palsy) and her 5 year old little sister with autism named Mia playing, but then Mia decides that she will become an edgy goth girl, despite the fact that she’s 5 years old, Mia can speak, but just started to speak, so her pronunciation isn’t the best and she knows a handful of words as of now, make sure you show when Mia mispronounces a word (which is very often), make sure Jane and Sam doesn’t appear but Izzy and Mia’s mother appears and make sure Mia is Izzy’s younger sister

Izzy and Mia are playing together in their playroom again. Mia is wearing a black shirt and a black skirt and has smeared black lipstick on her lips. Izzy looks at her younger sister with a slightly confused expression.

Izzy: (signing) "What are you dressed like that for, Mia?"

Mia: (verbally) "I'm an edgy goth girl now!"

Izzy: (signing) "What's a goth girl?"

Mia: (verbally) "It means I like dark stuff and spooky things."

Izzy nods, trying to understand. Their mother enters the room, looking surprised at Mia's appearance.
